task.title: Changing a hostname by using hostnamectl

context.p: You can use the hostnamectl utility to update the hostname.
By default, hostnamectl sets the following hostname types:

li.p: Static hostname: Stored in the /etc/hostname file. Typically, services use this name as the hostname.
Pretty hostname: A descriptive name, such as Proxy server in data center A.
Transient hostname: A fall-back value that is typically received from the network configuration.

step.cmd: Optional: Display the current hostname setting:

info.p: This command sets the static and transient hostname to the new value. To set only a specific type, pass the --static, --pretty, or --transient option to the command.

step.cmd: The hostnamectl utility automatically restarts the systemd-hostnamed to activate the new name. For the changes to take effect, reboot the host:

info.p: Alternatively, if you know which services use the hostname:

li.p: Restart all services that only read the hostname when the service starts:
Active shell users must re-login for the changes to take effect.
